hero.title: The Adventures of Robin Hood
hero.reason: In honor of National Technology Day, The Adventures of Robin Hood is chosen because it was one of the first major films to heavily utilize the newly developed three-strip Technicolor technology, which greatly enhanced the visual quality of the movie.
hero.blurb: This classic tale of the heroic outlaw Robin Hood is full of action, romance, and stunning visuals, making it a must-see for film enthusiasts. With its impressive use of early technology, it's a great way to celebrate National Technology Day. The movie's themes of bravery and justice are timeless and inspiring.

released.title: Hot Fuzz
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Hot Fuzz is an action-packed comedy that has become a cult classic, following the story of a top London cop who is sent to a small town to solve a series of mysterious accidents.
released.blurb: With its over-the-top action sequences, witty humor, and great performances, Hot Fuzz is an entertaining ride from start to finish. The film's clever parody of action movie tropes and its heartfelt portrayal of male friendship make it a standout in the comedy genre. It's a fun and exciting movie that will keep you laughing and on the edge of your seat.

birthday.title: A Few Good Men
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Tim Roth, A Few Good Men is one of his notable films, where he plays the role of Private First Class Louden Downey, a young Marine who is accused of murdering a fellow soldier.
birthday.blurb: A Few Good Men is a gripping drama that explores the complexities of military justice and the consequences of blind loyalty. With a talented cast, including Tom Cruise, Jack Nicholson, and Demi Moore, the film is a powerful and thought-provoking exploration of morality and honor.

awful.title: Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever is an action film that failed to impress critics and audiences alike with its convoluted plot and lackluster action sequences.
awful.blurb: This movie is a mess of poorly choreographed fight scenes, cringe-worthy dialogue, and a nonsensical plot that will leave you scratching your head. With a talentless cast and a complete lack of direction, Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever is a trainwreck that's so bad it's almost enjoyable.
